Our AI identifies nutritional deficiencies common in Kenya.' }, { icon:'fa-leaf', title:'Kenyan Food Guidance', desc:'Get recommendations for locally available, affordable foods — omena, sukuma wiki, ugali and more.' }, { icon:'fa-hospital', title:'Hospital Locator', desc:'Find verified healthcare facilities near you using OpenStreetMap. Search by name, city, or use GPS.' }, { icon:'fa-chart-line', title:'Health History', desc:'Track your analyses over time and monitor your nutritional health journey.' }, ]; const STATS = [ { value:'35%', label:'of Kenyan women have iron deficiency anaemia' }, { value:'1:5,000', label:'doctor-to-patient ratio in Kenya (WHO 2023)' }, { value:'26%', label:'of children under 5 are stunted due to poor nutrition' }, ]; const DEFICIENCIES = [ { icon:'fa-droplet', name:'Iron Anaemia', color:'#dc2626' }, { icon:'fa-eye', name:'Vitamin A', color:'#d97706' }, { icon:'fa-sun', name:'Vitamin D', color:'#f59e0b' }, { icon:'fa-brain', name:'Vitamin B12', color:'#0891b2' }, { icon:'fa-seedling', name:'Folate', color:'#16a34a' }, { icon:'fa-shield-halved', name:'Iodine', color:'#7c3aed' }, { icon:'fa-shield', name:'Zinc', color:'#0f766e' }, { icon:'fa-bone', name:'Calcium', color:'#64748b' }, { icon:'fa-lemon', name:'Vitamin C', color:'#ea580c' }, { icon:'fa-dumbbell', name:'Protein', color:'#b45309' }, ]; export default function Home() { return (
